commentary: Eight activities versus eleven — okay, yes, the volume dropped, but let's talk about what actually happened here, because that Saturday 13.3km around Mosman was not a small thing, and doing it off the back of two pool sessions and a weights day earlier in the week shows a kind of structured confidence that last week's slightly chaotic double-Balmoral-then-Virgin-then-everything energy didn't quite have. The running heart rate crept up from 149 to 155, which tracks — you were carrying more cumulative fatigue into Saturday this time, and you still went out and did it. The Tuesday closer is interesting too: 1500m at 130 bpm is relaxed, almost suspiciously relaxed, like you knew the week needed a soft landing and you actually gave yourself one, which is growth. Two proper gym days with named programs instead of "a bit of everything" also suggests your head is more in it this week than last, and honestly that is the kind of detail that deserves to be noticed.
metrics_commentary: Twenty and a half kilometres across just two run/walk sessions and six thousand two hundred and fifty metres in the pool — he's not dabbling, he's training, and that body knows it. The HRV is the real headline though: sixty-two milliseconds on the latest read against a seven-day average of fifty-one tells you his nervous system is recovering well and trending in exactly the right direction, which is the kind of detail that makes a himbo weep with pride. VO2 Max sitting at fifty-six point seven is genuinely strong — that's not a number you fake, that's aerobic capacity built by someone who keeps showing up. The only thing worth watching is whether two run sessions is enough stimulus to keep that engine growing, because a body this good deserves to be used.
gym_commentary: Two sessions out of four this week, babe — that's the honest part, and we're not going to dress it up in sequins it hasn't earned. Volume dropped from 24,518 kg down to 20,148 kg, and no new PRs landed, so this was a maintenance week at best and a quiet one at that. But here's what doesn't change regardless of how many times you showed up: a 168 kg leg press, a 107.9 kg trap bar deadlift, a back that pulls 84 kg, a chest pressing 76 kg on an incline — those numbers are just sitting there in your body, waiting, completely unbothered by a slow week. The strength didn't go anywhere; it's just resting, which honestly, same. Get back in there Thursday.
